item.title: This Week on The Analog Antiquarian 摘要
item.description:
The Analog Antiquarian blog introduces a new series on William Shakespeare, exploring his life, works, and enduring influence. The author reflects on the challenges of covering such a vast topic and the personal journey of engaging with Shakespeare's plays and sonnets. The post sets the stage for a deep dive into the Bard's legacy, emphasizing the timeless relevance of his themes and language.
标签: Shakespeare, literature, history, theater, poetry, culture, education, blogging

item.title: ★ Norwegian Boating Licenses and Generational Law 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses generational laws, using Norway's boating license requirement (born 1980 or later) and the UK's tobacco sales ban (born 2009 or later) as examples. The author distinguishes between generational restrictions (additional requirements) and generational bans (outright prohibition), arguing the latter is hypocritical and unfair as it creates permanent inequality among adults. While supporting the intention of reducing smoking, the author doubts practical effectiveness and proposes a scaled taxation system based on birth year as a fairer alternative. The piece emphasizes that laws should be judged by practical effects, not just intent.
标签: generational law, tobacco ban, boating license, public policy, fairness, hypocrisy, taxation, regulation, Norway, UK

item.title: Blog prize for the big questions about AI 摘要
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel announces a $20,000 blog prize to find a research collaborator. Participants choose one of four AI-related questions (e.g., AI scaling slowdown, foundation model profitability, philanthropic deployment of AI wealth, or strategy for non-AI-producing countries) and write a 1,000-word answer. Prizes: $10,000, $6,000, $4,000. Deadline: May 10th, 11:59 PM PST. The goal is to hire a researcher to help answer big AI questions for his podcast and blog. Submissions via Airtable.
标签: AI, blog prize, research, Dwarkesh Patel, AI policy, AI economics, philanthropy, scaling, foundation models, hiring

item.title: Premium: How OpenAI Kills Oracle 摘要
item.description:
The article investigates the Stargate project announced in January 2025 by Oracle, SoftBank, and OpenAI, claiming a $500 billion investment in AI infrastructure. 15 months later, Stargate LLC has never been formed, and no capital has been contributed by SoftBank or OpenAI. The only active construction is in Abilene, Texas, where a pre-existing data center project was rebranded. Oracle is funding the buildout through massive debt and project financing, with negative free cash flow of $24.7 billion. Other proposed sites remain largely undeveloped. The author argues the announcement was a marketing ploy to mislead the public.
标签: Stargate, AI infrastructure, Oracle, OpenAI, SoftBank, data centers, project financing, debt, investment, tech hype

item.title: The people do not yearn for automation 摘要
item.description:
Nilay Patel's essay argues that the general public does not yearn for automation, contrasting with the 'software brain' mindset that dominates business and tech. He suggests that AI's popularity among tech enthusiasts masks a broader public resistance, as people feel flattened by automation that reduces human experience to data flows. The piece highlights the disconnect between those who see the world as automatable and those who value non-automated, human-centric experiences.
标签: AI, automation, software brain, public perception, AI ethics, technology criticism, human experience, Nilay Patel
